biography: Matti etched his name into the records of the Hector Trophée through two distinct appearances. His engagement with this challenging pair competition was clear, despite his home club affiliations remaining unstated within tournament documentation.

During his two ventures onto the greens, Matti demonstrated a quiet dedication, consistently navigating the intricate demands of each round. While his diligent efforts were noted, neither the coveted Hector Trophée for his pairing nor the prestigious individual Victor Trophy ultimately materialized in his trophy cabinet, a testament to the exceptionally high standard of competition.

As the Hector Trophée 2026 approaches, Matti will, regrettably, not be among the competitors gracing the fairways. He has concluded his active involvement and is now considered retired from Hector events. Nevertheless, his time in the tournament, defined by a spirit of earnest competition, leaves a distinct, albeit brief, imprint on its history.
